summary: Backend-only testing for ResumePro ATS. 36 pytest tests covering auth, resumes CRUD, ATS scoring, jobs (public + employer), job applications w/ plan guard, Kanban tracker, mock PayUmoney payments, and all admin endpoints. Found and fixed 1 critical MongoDB index bug. All 36 tests pass (100%).

critical.issue: FIXED by testing agent. `resumes.share_token` had a unique+sparse index, but new resumes are inserted with `share_token: null`. `sparse` only skips MISSING fields, not null values, so creating any 2nd resume across the system raised DuplicateKeyError (500). Replaced with a partialFilterExpression index ({share_token: {$type: 'string'}}) and added a safe drop of the legacy `share_token_1` index on startup.

minor.issue: New jobs are created with is_active=false and only become active after mock job_post payment or admin verify. This is by design but may confuse employers; consider clearer API response message.
Response spreads raw user doc then adds counts; no explicit exclusion of sensitive fields beyond password_hash (already excluded). OK but tighten projection for future PII fields.
No signature/hash validation on the mock success endpoint (expected for mock). Any authenticated user can confirm their own pending txn. Flag for when real PayUmoney is wired.

action_items: Review the db.py index fix (partialFilterExpression on share_token) — verify acceptable on production Mongo. The legacy `share_token_1` index is auto-dropped on startup.
Consider not setting share_token:null on new resume docs (omit field until share is enabled) as a defence-in-depth alternative.
When wiring real PayUmoney, add hash/signature verification in /api/payments/payu-success.
critical_code_review_comments: db.py: share_token sparse-unique index was broken — fixed.
routes/resumes.py create_resume stores share_token:None by default; consider leaving it unset.
routes/payments.py payu-success trusts client-provided txn_id only (OK for mock, TODO for prod).

auth_utils.PLAN_FEATURES works correctly; require_feature + require_roles enforced across routes as tested (free blocked from canApplyJobs/shareLink/cloudDraft, limits enforced on ATS checks and maxResumes).
Admin endpoints correctly gated — non-admin gets 403 verified.
Admin actions are logged to admin_logs (grant_plan, verify_job, feature_job all verified).
Excluding Mongo _id projection is consistent across routes (good).
updated_files: /app/backend/db.py (fixed share_token unique index: partialFilterExpression + drop legacy)
test_credentials: admin@resumepro.in / Admin@ResumePro2026 (seeded). Test users registered dynamically with Test@1234 password, TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Seed users/resumes/jobs/applications created during tests use TEST_ prefix. Cleanup fixture removes them via admin delete endpoints at session end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green after the MongoDB index fix. Full pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (36 tests). Run: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_backend.py -v`. Admin grant-plan is how we upgrade users to jobseeker_pro in tests (no need to always go through mock payment). If /api/resumes returns 500 again, check backend logs for DuplicateKeyError on share_token — the index migration runs on backend startup.
rca of the issue: RCA: resumes collection had legacy unique+sparse index on share_token. MongoDB's `sparse` option only skips documents where the indexed field is MISSING, not where the value is null. The resume creation code explicitly sets share_token=None. So once ANY resume was inserted, every subsequent resume insert (any user, any resume) raised E11000 DuplicateKeyError on share_token:null. Reproduction: register user → POST /api/resumes twice → 2nd returns 500. Mitigation applied: replaced the index with partialFilterExpression {share_token: {$type: 'string'}} so only real share-tokens are enforced unique; legacy `share_token_1` index is dropped on startup. Verified with pytest: 36/36 pass.
